FILE: fetchRetailerCurrentPointBalance_steps.txt

DATE: 29 Oct 2025

AUTHOR: Srijita

PURPOSE: Step-by-step documentation of the fetchRetailerCurrentPointBalance API function

FUNCTION NAME:
fetchRetailerCurrentPointBalance

DESCRIPTION:

This API synchronizes the current RBP (Reward Bonus Point) balances of all active retailers (outlets) from an external RBP system API into the local database.

It retrieves live point balance data for each retailer, compares it against the local record (OutletApproved.currentPointBalance), and bulk updates any differences.

The function is engineered for performance, scalability, and data integrity with:


- Lock-based concurrency control

- Batch processing for large datasets

- Precision-based difference detection to prevent redundant writes

- Detailed logs and progress summaries

This ensures that each outlet’s reward point balance on the local server mirrors the latest data from the external RBP API.

STEP 1: Acquire Lock to Prevent Concurrent Execution

Lock Definition:
const lockKey = "updateOutletBalances";

if (!(await acquireLock(lockKey))) {
  res.status(400);
  throw new Error("Another sync is in progress. Please try again later.");
}

Purpose:


- Prevents multiple simultaneous executions of balance sync jobs.

- Ensures atomicity and eliminates race conditions when writing to the same collection.

Logging Example:
console.log("🚀 Starting outlet balance update process...");

Lock Cleanup:

Always released in the finally block to guarantee that it doesn’t block future runs:

STEP 2: Call External API to Fetch Retailer Balances

API Request:

Expected Response Structure:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "retailer_id": "653fb5bcd192fa70cddee222",
      "wallet_balance": "442.50"
    },
    ...
  ]
}

Extract Result:
const balanceData = response?.data?.data || [];
if (!balanceData || balanceData.length === 0) {
  return res.status(404).json({
    error: true,
    message: "No balance data received from API",
  });
}

Purpose:

- Fetches all current wallet balances for retailers from the external RBP system.

- Validates and ensures non-empty data before proceeding.

STEP 3: Build Retailer Balance Lookup Map

Create an In-Memory Balance Map:

const balanceMap = new Map();
balanceData.forEach((item) => {
  if (item.retailer_id && item.wallet_balance !== undefined) {
    balanceMap.set(
      item.retailer_id.toString(),
      parseFloat(item.wallet_balance) || 0
    );
  }
});

Purpose:

- Converts the fetched array into a quick-access map for constant-time lookups.

- Maps each retailer’s unique _id to their up-to-date point balance.

Logging Example:
console.log(`📋 Created balance map with ${balanceMap.size} entries`);

STEP 4: Set Up Batch Processing Parameters


Large outlet data sets are processed in chunks to optimize memory and performance.

Batch Configuration:
const BATCH_SIZE = 2000; // 2000 outlets per batch
let totalProcessed = 0;
let totalUpdated = 0;
let totalSkipped = 0;
let skip = 0;
let batchNumber = 1;

Purpose:


- Enables incremental database fetching and writing.

- Prevents high memory usage by limiting each processing loop to 2,000 records.

STEP 5: Process Outlets Batch-by-Batch

Batch Loop:
while (true) {
  console.log(`🔄 Processing batch ${batchNumber} (${skip} to ${skip + BATCH_SIZE})`);

  const outlets = await OutletApproved.find({ status: true })
    .select("_id currentPointBalance")
    .skip(skip)
    .limit(BATCH_SIZE)
    .lean();

  if (outlets.length === 0) {
    console.log("✅ All batches processed");
    break;
  }

  // Processing logic below...
}

Purpose:

- Fetches outlets in paginated manner.

- Ensures that inactive outlets (status: false) are excluded.

STEP 6: Compare and Prepare Bulk Operations

For each outlet, check if the new balance differs from the one stored locally:
const bulkOperations = [];
outlets.forEach((outlet) => {
  totalProcessed++;
  
  const outletId = outlet._id.toString();
  const newBalance = balanceMap.get(outletId);
  
  // Skip if external balance not found
  if (!balanceMap.has(outletId)) {
    totalSkipped++;
    return;
  }

  const currentBalance = outlet.currentPointBalance || 0;

  // Compare with precision tolerance to prevent unnecessary updates
  if (Math.abs(newBalance - currentBalance) > 0.0001) {
    bulkOperations.push({
      updateOne: {
        filter: { _id: outlet._id },
        update: { $set: { currentPointBalance: newBalance } },
      },
    });
  } else {
    totalSkipped++;
  }
});

Purpose:


- Minimizes redundant writes to maintain performance and reduce load.

- Accurately tracks updated vs. skipped records.

Precision Check:

Balances are compared up to 4 decimal places using a tolerance of 0.0001.

STEP 7: Execute Bulk Update Using MongoDB bulkWrite()

Perform Bulk Update:
if (bulkOperations.length > 0) {
  try {
    const bulkResult = await OutletApproved.bulkWrite(bulkOperations, { ordered: false });
    totalUpdated += bulkResult.modifiedCount;
    console.log(
      `✅ Batch ${batchNumber} completed - Updated: ${bulkResult.modifiedCount} outlets`
    );
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`❌ Bulk write failed for batch ${batchNumber}:`, error.message);
  }
} else {
  console.log(`⏭️ Batch ${batchNumber} - No updates needed`);
}

Purpose:


- Executes high-performance, batched writes to MongoDB in unsequenced (“unordered”) mode for efficiency.

- All successful updates within the batch are committed even if some fail.

STEP 8: Advance Batch and Introduce Controlled Delay

After each batch:
skip += BATCH_SIZE;
batchNumber++;
await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 200)); // Delay 200ms

Purpose:


- Prevents potential deadlocks or overloads on MongoDB from continuous bulk operations.

- Introduces a small throttling delay (200ms) between batches for system stability.

STEP 9: Aggregate Final Statistics and Log Summary

When all batches finish:
const totalTime = Date.now() - startTime;
console.log("\n" + "=".repeat(50));
console.log("📊 BALANCE UPDATE SUMMARY");
console.log("=".repeat(50));
console.log(`⏱️  Total Time: ${(totalTime / 1000).toFixed(2)}s`);
console.log(`📋 Total Processed: ${totalProcessed}`);
console.log(`✅ Total Updated: ${totalUpdated}`);
console.log(`⏭️  Total Skipped: ${totalSkipped}`);
console.log(
  `📈 Success Rate: ${
    totalProcessed > 0 ? ((totalUpdated / totalProcessed) * 100).toFixed(2) : 0
  }%`
);
console.log("=".repeat(50) + "\n");

Purpose:


- Provides a complete runtime summary including:
	- Execution time in seconds
	- Total records processed
	- Update/skip ratios
	- Success percentage

STEP 10: Return Final API Response

Response:

res.status(200).json({
  error: false,
  message: "Outlet balance update completed successfully",
  data: {
    totalProcessed,
    totalUpdated,
    totalSkipped,
    executionTime: totalTime,
    successRate:
      totalProcessed > 0
        ? ((totalUpdated / totalProcessed) * 100).toFixed(2)
        : 0,
    balanceRecordsReceived: balanceData.length,
    balanceEntriesMapped: balanceMap.size,
  },
});

Sample Output:
{
  "error": false,
  "message": "Outlet balance update completed successfully",
  "data": {
    "totalProcessed": 32000,
    "totalUpdated": 4500,
    "totalSkipped": 27500,
    "executionTime": 42500,
    "successRate": "14.06",
    "balanceRecordsReceived": 32000,
    "balanceEntriesMapped": 31998
  }
}

Purpose:

- Returns a clear operational summary to the client/application with all key metrics.

- Helps in monitoring and verifying synchronization success rate.

STEP 11: Error Handling & Cleanup

Error Handling:
catch (error) {
  console.error("❌ Balance update failed:", error.message);
  res.status(400);
  throw error;
}

Finally Block:
finally {
  await releaseLock(lockKey);
  console.log("🔓 Balance update lock released");
}

Purpose:


- Ensures lock release even if API fails or exceptions occur mid-process.

- Maintains system integrity and future availability of the synchronization task.

FINAL OUTCOME


When executed successfully:


1. Retrieves the latest RBP balances for all retailers from the external system.

2. Updates each retailer’s currentPointBalance if the local value differs.

3. Executes updates in optimized batched operations.

4. Provides a detailed performance summary.

5. Ensures only one synchronization job runs at a time to maintain consistency.

SUMMARY OF LOGICAL FLOW

1. Acquire lock: Prevent concurrent balance update jobs.

2. Fetch RBP balances from the external API.

3. Validate response and ensure usable data.

4. Map balances to retailer IDs for fast lookup.

5. Batch process outlets in chunks of 2000 records.

6. Compare balances and create bulk update operations for mismatches.

7. Execute bulk writes to MongoDB.

8. Aggregate results for processed, updated, and skipped items.

9. Return summary report with metrics and execution time.

10. Release lock post-execution (always).

IMPORTANT BUSINESS RULES

1. Single-thread protection: Only one balance update job runs concurrently (updateOutletBalances lock).

2. Data source: Real-time RBP balances fetched from external configuration endpoint (ALL_RETAILER_CURRENT_RBP_POINT_BALANCE).

3. Precision comparison: Updates performed only for changes > ±0.0001 points.

4. Scalability: Processes retail data in controlled batches of 2000 for optimal efficiency.

5. Fault tolerance: Partial failures (e.g., network or write errors) are logged without stopping the global process.

6. Speed limiting: Introduces a 200ms delay between batches to prevent database overload.

7. Status tracking: Returns detailed metrics for monitoring system performance.

8. Consistency: Each valid outlet’s point balance remains synchronized with RBP service.

9. Safety guarantee: Lock is released no matter the outcome, ensuring future jobs always proceed safely.

10. Monitoring support: Logs contain batch progress, timing, and success rates for transparent job auditing.

AUTHOR: Srijita
DATE: 29 Oct 2025
